commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d0f13a1d64d9feaca6c14abaf4b4b6b75b3c2029)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads inferred or explicit cron wake `sessionKey`/`agentId` values from the cron tool through gateway wake validation and `CronService.wake`, with focused wake/protocol tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current main's cron tool wake path still calls the gateway with only `{mode, text}`, while the gateway/cron service already have session-key-aware wake handling. I did not run tests because this review is explicitly read-only.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs contributor or maintainer rebase plus real behavior proof; automated repair should not be requested while external real proof is missing.

target.title: fix(cron): capture originating session/agent on the cron wake tool call
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** The cron `wake` MCP tool forwards only `{mode, text}` to the gateway. With no `sessionKey` / `agentId` reaching `context.cron.wake()`, every wake enqueues a system event against the heartbeat / main default — so wakes scheduled from a non-main session (a Telegram thread, a Discord channel, a multi-agent setup) silently route to the wrong conversation lane.
- **Why it matters.** On CLI runtimes the fired wake spawns a fresh session and burns tokens generating output the caller has no way to route back. On embedded the wake fires the agent's default session, not the originating one. Cost-relevant; user-visibly broken across every channel.
- **What changed.** The wake tool now resolves `opts.agentSessionKey` through the existing `resolveInternalSessionKey` / `resolveSessionAgentId` pattern (mirroring the `add` action at L569-583) and threads `sessionKey` + `agentId` through `WakeParamsSchema` → gateway handler → `context.cron.wake` → `enqueueSystemEvent` + `requestHeartbeatNow`. Explicit params take precedence over inferred values.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Schema's `additionalProperties: true` is unchanged (forward-compat metadata still passes through). Default fallback for omitted origin is unchanged (pre-existing call sites without `sessionKey`/`agentId` still route to the heartbeat / main default). Channel/thread/topic capture on the job's `delivery` block is **out of scope** — that's a follow-up once this contract lands.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** the cron `wake` MCP tool, when invoked by an agent inside a non-main session, now threads the originating session/agent through the wake gateway call so the system event lands on that session's queue (and the requested heartbeat — on `mode: "now"` — drives the originating lane).
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, AJV-compiled schema validators, vitest 4.1.6.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run src/cron/service/wake-origin.test.ts src/gateway/protocol/index.test.ts`; `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.core.json` (no new errors introduced; pre-existing `@mariozechner/pi-agent-core` / `pi-coding-agent` package-not-installed errors are unrelated to this PR's diff).
- **Evidence after fix:**

  ```
   RUN  v4.1.6 C:/Users/Cameron/clawd/openclaw-fork

   Test Files  3 passed (3)
        Tests  35 passed (35)
     Start at  20:37:20
     Duration  6.28s
  ```

  Includes the 6 new `wake-origin.test.ts` tests and the 2 new `validateWakeParams` cases for the optional fields.
- **Observed result after fix:** `wake({mode, text, sessionKey, agentId})` calls `enqueueSystemEvent(text, {sessionKey, agentId})` rather than `enqueueSystemEvent(text)`. `mode: "now"` additionally drives `requestHeartbeatNow({reason: "wake", sessionKey, agentId})` so the targeted session's run loop wakes (instead of the heartbeat / main default). Whitespace-only or omitted origin falls through to the pre-fix default — backwards compatible.
- **What was not tested:** live end-to-end with a real Telegram thread → cron wake → delivery, because that exercises downstream delivery routing (out of scope here); deliberately split so this PR can land on its own merit. Subsequent PR will plumb channel/thread/topic capture on `job.delivery`.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Issues: closes the upstream half of #46886 (cron wake action always routes to default agent) and #64556 (hooks.mappings[].agentId and sessionKey silently ignored for action='wake').
- Related PRs (downstream slices of the same wake-routing problem): #70268, #57199, #82767, #79869, #63096. This PR fixes the upstream origin-capture all of them silently assume.

## Root Cause

`src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts` wake action only sends `{mode, text}` to the gateway. `src/cron/service/timer.ts:wake()` calls `state.deps.enqueueSystemEvent(text)` with no options, so the underlying bound implementation falls back to whatever default sessionKey it was constructed with (heartbeat / main). The `add` action at L569-583 already had the right pattern for resolving the originating session — wake just hadn't picked it up.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `src/cron/service/wake-origin.test.ts` (new) and `src/gateway/protocol/index.test.ts` (extended).
- **Scenario locked in:**
  1. `wake({mode: "now", sessionKey, agentId})` calls `enqueueSystemEvent(text, {sessionKey, agentId})` and `requestHeartbeatNow({reason: "wake", sessionKey, agentId})`.
  2. `mode: "next-heartbeat"` enqueues but does NOT call `requestHeartbeatNow`.
  3. Origin omitted → default routing preserved (backwards compatible).
  4. Whitespace-only origin → treated as omitted (defence in depth).
  5. Empty `text` still rejected (no regression of pre-fix guard).
  6. `validateWakeParams` accepts the new optional fields and rejects empty strings.
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode is at the `wake()` function boundary — full state machine isn't needed. Mocked-deps unit tests run in ms vs the minutes a live cron-fire e2e would cost.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** the wake action's gateway call shape changes; existing in-process callers that send `wake({mode, text})` without origin must keep working.
  - **Mitigation:** `sessionKey` and `agentId` are optional. When omitted (or whitespace-only), wake falls through to the deps default — identical to the pre-fix behaviour. Regression test pins this branch.
- **Risk:** the schema becomes too strict by typing `sessionKey`/`agentId` as `NonEmptyString` — existing callers sending empty strings would suddenly fail validation.
  - **Mitigation:** existing callers don't send these fields at all (only the new tool-side wiring populates them). The schema's `additionalProperties: true` is unchanged so unknown fields still pass; the typed slots only validate when the field is present.
- **Risk:** the inferred `agentId` could differ from the actual job's `agentId` in multi-agent setups where the calling agent owns the schedule but a different agent should run the wake.
  - **Mitigation:** explicit `sessionKey` / `agentId` params on the tool call take precedence. Cross-agent wakes remain expressible. The implicit capture is a sensible default for the overwhelming case ("wake my own session").

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** No.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — session keys and agent ids are not credentials.
- **New/changed network calls:** No.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `wake` action; only the routing target changes.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — wake events are still confined to in-process session queues. Routing now goes to the correct queue rather than the default queue.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backwards compatible. Pre-existing callers that send `wake({mode, text})` continue to work via the dep's default routing.
- No new config, no new env vars.
- Migration needed: no.

## Out of scope (deliberate split)

- Capture of `channel`, `threadId`, `topicId` on the job's `delivery` block so the wake's REPLY routes back to the originating Telegram thread / Discord channel / Slack thread / etc. That's a follow-up that builds on this contract: once `sessionKey` reaches the cron service via wake, `inferDeliveryFromSessionKey` (already used by the `add` action at L616) can derive the delivery target downstream.
- CLI runtime's `--resume <session>` plumbing on cron-triggered runs. The CLI runner already consumes `useResume: true` + `resumeSessionKey` from `runCliAgent` params; the cron isolated-agent runner just needs to populate those from the captured `sessionKey`. Out of scope here to keep the diff focused on the universal origin-capture.
